title: Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS) Deepens AI Data Center Ties With Nvidia
text: Navitas Semiconductor's CEO Chris Allexandre presented at the 28th Annual Needham Growth Conference in mid-January 2026, highlighting the company's strategic shift toward power semiconductors for artificial intelligence data centers. The company is focusing on higher-power gallium nitride and silicon carbide chips, with particular emphasis on collaboration with Nvidia's emerging 800V architecture platform. This tie-in directly connects the conference appearance to the key potential growth driver for the business. The narrative requires investors to believe that Navitas can transition successfully from struggling mobile and consumer markets into profitable AI data center operations. Current projections suggest the company aims to reach $129.8 million in revenue by 2028, requiring approximately 24% annual growth. However, challenges persist, with ongoing revenue declines and cost cutting as the company works through weakness in EV, solar, and industrial segments while pursuing AI opportunities. Simply Wall St analysts value the stock at $8.28 per share, suggesting potential downside from current trading levels, indicating significant execution risk remains for this transformation story.
